title: WAF Edge Demo
subtitle: Real HTTP requests adapted from public WAF corpora, then routed through explicit plugins and grouped pearls.
tagline: The engine stays generic. HTTP parsing and WAF semantics live in adapters and plugins. The route logic stays inspectable.

metrics.label: Demo Slice
metrics.note: Exact route matches on the checked-in real-data slice

metrics.label: Benchmark Corpus
metrics.note: Mixed request-level cases from CSIC 2010 and OWASP ModSecurity 2025

metrics.label: Decision Families
metrics.note: Injection payloads, restricted resources, and suspicious automation

metrics.label: Artifact Shape
metrics.note: Versionable bundle for teams, not an opaque binary

artifacts.label: Injection Payload Pearl
artifacts.description: Deterministic denial logic for SQLi, XSS, and traversal-style payloads.

artifacts.label: Sensitive Surface Pearl
artifacts.description: Deterministic denial logic for restricted-resource access from untrusted traffic.

artifacts.label: Route Pearl
artifacts.description: Collective route pearl that decides allow, deny, or review.

artifacts.label: Observer Plugin
artifacts.description: Thin WAF observer that reads request fields and plugin-owned pattern config.

artifacts.label: WAF Benchmark Config
artifacts.description: Route-family mapping stays in WAF-owned config, not in the generic engine.

principles: The generic engine owns deterministic execution, pearls, benchmark plumbing, and inspection.
HTTP parsing lives in Rust benchmark adapters so the public benchmark path does not depend on ad hoc Python parsing.
WAF meaning stays in plugins and WAF-owned config files, not in the generic core crates.
The checked-in walkthrough cases are generated from the larger public corpora so the demo stays readable without becoming fake.

cases.title: Allow a normal storefront request
cases.summary: A routine application request from the CSIC corpus stays on the allow path with no injection or restricted-resource signals.

cases.title: Block a SQL injection request
cases.summary: A real anomalous CSIC request carries a time-delay SQL injection payload and is blocked by the injection pearl.
cases.counterfactual: Remove the exploit payload and keep the request parameters in their expected application format.

cases.title: Block restricted-resource access
cases.summary: A real ModSecurity audit-log request for a restricted configuration file is denied before application logic can see it.
cases.counterfactual: Keep public traffic off restricted files and move privileged resource access behind trusted boundaries.

headers.user-agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/91.0.4472.124 Safari/537.36

cases.title: Review suspicious automation
cases.summary: A real ModSecurity bad-bot request is routed to review rather than treated as a direct exploit family match.
cases.counterfactual: Use standard browser-style requests and remove scanner fingerprints or malformed probe behavior.
